assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.
The recognised intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a foreign-currency-denominated intragroup monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised receivable is a recognised asset, so the recognised-item hedge model is a relevant peer IFRS hedge-accounting model for assessing FX risk hedging in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately recognises the forecast-transaction cash flow hedge model, which is relevant as the peer alternative to a recognised-item hedge when analysing dividend-related FX exposures.
